overview.brief_description: Kaunas, Lithuania's second-largest city, is a vibrant cultural and historical hub nestled at the confluence of the Nemunas and Neris rivers. Known for its rich interwar architecture and dynamic arts scene, Kaunas effortlessly blends tradition with modern urban energy.
why_visit: Experience a thriving cultural and arts scene
Discover medieval and historic landmarks like Kaunas Castle
Enjoy vibrant cafes, festivals, and riverfront promenades
overview.best_time_to_visit: May to September, when the weather is warm and outdoor festivals abound

seasonal_notes.spring: Mild temperatures with blossoming parks and outdoor cafes opening up.
seasonal_notes.summer: Warm and sunny, perfect for river cruises and festivals like the Kaunas Jazz Festival.
seasonal_notes.autumn: Cooler weather with beautiful fall colors in city parks and fewer tourists.
seasonal_notes.winter: Cold and snowy, offering cozy atmospheres in local museums and cafes, plus festive Christmas markets.

practical_info.visit_duration: 2 to 3 days to explore main attractions and cultural sites
practical_info.getting_around: Efficient bus and trolleybus network, bike rentals, and walkable city center
practical_info.safety: Generally very safe with low crime rates; usual European city precautions advised

must_do: Visit Kaunas Old Town and the historic Kaunas Castle
Explore the interwar modernist architecture district
Discover the Devil’s Museum with its unique collection
Stroll along the Nemunas River promenade
Climb to the top of the Žaliakalnis Funicular for panoramic views
worth_doing: Visit the Ninth Fort Museum to learn about Lithuania’s history
Enjoy contemporary art at the M.K. Čiurlionis National Art Museum
Relax in the expansive Ąžuolynas Park
Attend a performance at the Kaunas State Musical Theatre
Explore the vibrant street art scene around the city center
hidden_gems: Check out the quirky Pazaislis Monastery, a Baroque architectural masterpiece outside the city center
Discover the unique ceramics workshops and galleries in the city
Take a quiet walk along the smaller, less touristy Šančiai neighborhood by the river

sites.name: Kaunas Castle
sites.description: Kaunas Castle is a medieval fortress dating back to the 14th century, located at the confluence of the Nemunas and Neris rivers. It played a pivotal role in defending the Grand Duchy of Lithuania and now hosts exhibitions and cultural events.
sites.seasonal_notes: Outdoor areas best enjoyed in spring to autumn; winter may limit access
sites.booking_tips: Tickets available onsite; guided tours recommended during summer

opening_hours.notes: Closed on Mondays from November to March

visit_profile.weather_fallback: M. K. Čiurlionis National Art Museum

sites.name: Vytautas the Great War Museum
sites.description: This museum showcases Lithuania's military history, from medieval times to modern conflicts, featuring extensive collections of weapons, uniforms, and artifacts. The adjacent Peace Monument and the impressive building itself add to the experience.
sites.booking_tips: Advance booking recommended for group tours

sites.name: Laisvės Alėja (Liberty Avenue)
sites.description: Laisvės Alėja is the longest pedestrian street in Eastern Europe, lined with cafes, shops, and historic buildings. It’s a vibrant artery of Kaunas life and a great place for strolling, people-watching, and sampling local cuisine.
sites.seasonal_notes: Bustling in summer and during festivals; quieter in winter

sites.name: M. K. Čiurlionis National Art Museum
sites.description: Dedicated to the works of the renowned Lithuanian painter and composer Mikalojus Konstantinas Čiurlionis, this museum also features Lithuanian and international art spanning several centuries. Its classical architecture adds to the cultural atmosphere.
sites.booking_tips: Tickets can be purchased onsite; guided tours available

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Perfect indoor alternative to outdoor tours

sites.name: Pažaislis Monastery and Church
sites.description: This stunning Baroque monastery complex, located slightly outside the city center along the Kaunas Reservoir, is famed for its intricate frescoes, elegant architecture, and tranquil gardens. It often hosts classical music concerts in the summer.
sites.seasonal_notes: Concerts mainly in summer; gardens best in late spring and summer
sites.booking_tips: Entry free; concert tickets sold separately

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Visit the nearby Kaunas Reservoir indoor exhibition

sites.name: Devils' Museum (Kauko muziejus)
sites.description: Unique to Kaunas, this quirky museum houses a collection of over 3,000 devil-themed artifacts from various cultures. The exhibits range from sculptures to masks, showcasing folklore, humor, and artistic creativity.
sites.booking_tips: Small venue; no advance booking needed

sites.name: Nemunas Island Park
sites.description: A large recreational area situated on an island formed by the Nemunas River, this park offers walking and cycling paths, playgrounds, and open spaces for picnics. It’s a popular spot for locals and visitors seeking nature and relaxation within the city.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best in spring through autumn; limited access during floods

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Visit nearby indoor cafes or museums

sites.name: Žalgiris Arena
sites.description: The largest multifunctional arena in the Baltics, home to the Žalgiris basketball team and hosting concerts, exhibitions, and major events. The sleek modern architecture and lively atmosphere make it a key landmark of contemporary Kaunas.
sites.booking_tips: Book tickets for games and concerts in advance online

sites.name: Kaunas Town Hall (White Swan)
sites.description: Known locally as the White Swan, this elegant 16th-century Renaissance building served as the city’s administrative center. Today, it houses a small museum and is a popular wedding venue, located in the heart of Old Town.
sites.booking_tips: Visit early to avoid crowds; check for special exhibitions

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Perfect indoor stop on rainy days

sites.name: Kaunas Botanical Garden
sites.description: Covering over 60 hectares, this botanical garden features thousands of plant species, thematic collections, and greenhouses. It’s a peaceful retreat for nature lovers and a center for botanical research and education.
sites.seasonal_notes: Greenhouse is ideal in winter; outdoor gardens best in spring to autumn
sites.booking_tips: Tickets can be purchased onsite; group tours available

visit_profile.weather_fallback: Visit greenhouses if cold or rainy

sites.name: Kaunas Philharmonic
sites.description: A premier venue for classical music performances, the Kaunas Philharmonic hosts local and international artists in a beautifully restored historic building. The program includes symphony orchestras, chamber concerts, and solo recitals.
sites.seasonal_notes: Concert season peaks in autumn and winter
sites.booking_tips: Advance ticket purchase recommended, especially for popular events

sites.name: Kaunas Zoo
sites.description: Established in 1938, Kaunas Zoo is home to over 2,000 animals representing more than 200 species. The zoo focuses on conservation and education, offering family-friendly exhibits and interactive experiences.
sites.seasonal_notes: Some animals less visible in winter; spring to autumn most active
sites.booking_tips: Tickets available onsite; arrive early to see all exhibits

sites.name: Aleksotas Funicular
sites.description: Opened in 1935, this historic funicular railway connects the city center with the Aleksotas district across the Nemunas River. It offers panoramic views of the city skyline and is a charming way to explore Kaunas’ varied neighborhoods.
sites.seasonal_notes: Operates year-round with occasional maintenance closures
sites.booking_tips: Tickets purchased onboard; small queues usually

sites.name: Open-Air Museum of the 20th Century
sites.description: This outdoor museum displays Soviet-era architecture, household items, and vehicles, providing insight into Lithuania's recent history. It’s an immersive experience that contextualizes the cultural and political shifts of the 20th century.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best visited in warmer months; limited access in winter

sites.name: Central Market (Kauno Centrinis Turgus)
sites.description: Offering fresh produce, local delicacies, and artisan goods, the Central Market is a lively spot to experience everyday Kaunas life. The market hall’s modernist architecture adds historic charm to the bustling atmosphere.
sites.seasonal_notes: Seasonal produce changes throughout the year

sites.name: Romainiai Old Jewish Cemetery
sites.description: One of the oldest Jewish cemeteries in Lithuania, this solemn site reflects the rich Jewish cultural heritage of Kaunas before World War II. It’s a place for quiet reflection and historical remembrance.
sites.seasonal_notes: Accessible year-round; peaceful in all seasons
sites.booking_tips: Respectful behavior required; no guided tours usually

sites.name: Devil's Museum Gift Shop
sites.description: Located next to the Devils' Museum, this gallery offers a curated selection of devil-themed souvenirs and folk art. It’s an excellent place to find unique gifts inspired by Lithuanian mythology.

sites.name: Cathedral Basilica of Kaunas
sites.description: The largest Gothic church in Lithuania, the Cathedral Basilica is an architectural masterpiece with origins from the 15th century. Its towering spires and richly decorated interior make it a highlight for visitors in the Old Town.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open year-round; special masses during religious holidays

neighborhoods.name: Old Town (Senamiestis)
neighborhoods.character: Kaunas Old Town is a charming historic district where cobblestone streets wind past medieval walls, vibrant cafes, and baroque architecture. It offers a romantic and timeless vibe blending lively tourist energy with deep historical roots.

location.description: Located in the heart of the city along the confluence of the Nemunas and Neris rivers.

history.overview: Senamiestis has been the core of Kaunas since medieval times, once a strategic trading hub and defensive site.
history.significance: It preserves Kaunas' architectural heritage and is the cultural and historical soul of the city.
notable_events: Medieval establishment and construction of Kaunas Castle in the 14th century
World War II damage and post-war restoration

practical_info.safety: Generally safe, with usual urban precautions at night.
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring through early autumn for outdoor cafes and festivals

attractions.name: Kaunas Castle
attractions.description: A 14th-century Gothic castle guarding the river confluence and hosting exhibitions.
attractions.appeal: Explore medieval history and scenic river views.

attractions.name: Town Hall Square
attractions.description: The vibrant central square featuring the iconic ‘White Swan’ Town Hall building.
attractions.appeal: Great for people-watching and nearby cafes.

insider_tips: Visit early morning to enjoy the quiet charm before tourists arrive.
Check out the art galleries hidden in side streets for local contemporary art.
Walk along the Nemunas riverside promenade for peaceful views.

neighborhoods.name: Centras
neighborhoods.character: Centras is the bustling commercial and administrative hub with a mix of modern offices, shops, and entertainment venues. The atmosphere is dynamic and cosmopolitan, perfect for urban explorers seeking lively streets and conveniences.

location.description: Just east of Old Town, encompassing the main business and shopping district.

history.overview: Developed primarily in the 19th and 20th centuries as Kaunas expanded beyond its medieval core.
history.significance: It represents the modern growth and commercial heart of Kaunas.
notable_events: Construction of Laisvės Alėja as a pedestrian boulevard

practical_info.safety: Safe with active police presence and many pedestrians day and night.
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Year-round, especially during festivals and shopping seasons
practical_info.walkability: Very walkable with wide pedestrian streets and public transport options

attractions.name: Laisvės Alėja
attractions.description: Longest pedestrian street in Eastern Europe lined with shops, cafes, and theaters.
attractions.appeal: Ideal for strolling, shopping, and people-watching.

dining.name: Višta Puode
dining.known_for: Comfort food with a modern twist

insider_tips: Explore side streets off Laisvės Alėja for boutique shops and quieter cafes.
Visit during the Christmas Market for festive lights and local crafts.
Use bike rentals to explore the broad avenues efficiently.

neighborhoods.name: Žaliakalnis
neighborhoods.character: Žaliakalnis is a leafy, upscale residential area known for its parks, historic villas, and relaxed pace. It offers a peaceful, green retreat from the city's bustle with a refined yet approachable vibe.

location.description: North of the city center, on a gentle hill overlooking Kaunas.

history.overview: Developed in the early 20th century as a garden suburb with a focus on nature and health.
history.significance: Represents Kaunas’ interwar modernist aspirations and love of green urban spaces.
notable_events: Construction of the Žaliakalnis Funicular in 1931
Development of many modernist villas during the interwar period

transit: Several bus lines connecting to city center
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring and summer for park visits and outdoor activities

attractions.name: Kaunas Botanical Garden
attractions.description: Sprawling gardens showcasing native and exotic plants.
attractions.appeal: Perfect for nature walks and plant enthusiasts.

attractions.name: Žaliakalnis Funicular
attractions.description: A vintage funicular connecting the hilltop residential area with the city below.
attractions.appeal: Unique transport experience with scenic views.

insider_tips: Visit early morning at the botanical garden to avoid crowds.
Take the funicular for a nostalgic ride and photo opportunities.
Explore the side streets to admire Art Deco and modernist villas.

neighborhoods.name: Aleksotas
neighborhoods.character: Aleksotas is a vibrant residential neighborhood with a mix of Soviet-era apartment blocks and natural beauty near the river. It has a local, lived-in feel with growing interest for outdoor activities and river views.

location.description: South of the Old Town, across the Nemunas river.

history.overview: Once a separate suburb, Aleksotas was integrated into Kaunas during the 20th century and developed mainly during Soviet times.
history.significance: Offers insight into Kaunas’ urban expansion and Soviet architectural legacy.
notable_events: Integration into Kaunas city limits in the 20th century
Development of Aleksotas Funicular in the 1930s

practical_info.safety: Generally safe though less touristy; standard urban precautions advised.
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Late spring to early autumn for river walks and views

attractions.name: Aleksotas Observation Deck
attractions.description: Offers panoramic views of the Old Town across the river.
attractions.appeal: Photographers’ favorite spot for sunset and cityscapes.

insider_tips: Walk up to the observation deck for the best sunset views over Kaunas.
Use the funicular for a quick and scenic connection to the city center.
Visit local markets for authentic Lithuanian products away from tourist crowds.

neighborhoods.name: Laisvės Alėja Food District
neighborhoods.character: This vibrant zone along Laisvės Alėja is a culinary hotspot featuring diverse eateries, trendy cafes, and lively food markets. It buzzes with gastronomic creativity and is the go-to place for food lovers craving authentic and international flavors.

location.description: Centered along the pedestrian Laisvės Alėja street in Centras.

history.overview: Developed around the main pedestrian artery, this area evolved into a dining hub in recent decades.
history.significance: It reflects Kaunas’ modern culinary trends and social culture.
notable_events: Establishment of the pedestrian Laisvės Alėja boulevard
Emergence of food festivals and markets in the 2000s

practical_info.safety: Very safe and well-patrolled, popular among locals and tourists.
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Evenings and weekends for vibrant dining and nightlife

attractions.name: Kaunas Food Market
attractions.description: A lively market offering local produce, street food, and artisan goods.
attractions.appeal: Perfect spot to sample authentic Lithuanian flavors.

dining.name: Etno Dvaras
dining.known_for: Authentic national dishes in a rustic setting

insider_tips: Try the seasonal food festivals for unique tasting experiences.
Reserve tables on weekends as places fill up quickly.
Explore the side alleys for hidden gem cafes and dessert shops.

neighborhoods.name: Vilijampolė
neighborhoods.character: Vilijampolė is a historic and diverse neighborhood on the west bank of the Nemunas river with a multicultural past. It has a gritty yet authentic urban feel, increasingly popular with artists and young residents.

location.description: West of the Old Town, across the Nemunas river.

history.overview: Historically a working-class district with a significant Jewish community before WWII.
history.significance: It reflects Kaunas’ multicultural heritage and post-war transformations.
notable_events: Pre-WWII Jewish cultural and commercial center
WWII and Holocaust impacts on the community

practical_info.safety: Generally safe during the day; standard urban caution advised at night.
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring and summer for riverside walks and festivals
practical_info.walkability: Moderate walkability with some industrial zones

attractions.name: Nemunas Island
attractions.description: A peaceful island park offering walking trails and picnic spots.
attractions.appeal: Great for outdoor relaxation close to the city.

insider_tips: Explore the riverside paths for serene views and birdwatching.
Visit local galleries showcasing contemporary Lithuanian art.
Try traditional snacks from small family-run cafes off the main roads.

fine_dining.name: Uoksas
fine_dining.description: Innovative tasting menus spotlighting local ingredients with a contemporary twist.
fine_dining.atmosphere: Intimate and elegant with minimalist decor
fine_dining.local_tips: Try their seasonal mushroom dishes, especially in autumn.

fine_dining.name: Monai
fine_dining.description: A Michelin-starred restaurant offering refined dishes with an emphasis on local produce.
fine_dining.atmosphere: Sophisticated and modern with artistic plating
fine_dining.local_tips: Reserve the chef’s tasting menu paired with Lithuanian wines.

fine_dining.name: Bernelių Užeiga
fine_dining.description: Classic Lithuanian dishes served in a cozy, wood-paneled setting, highlighting Kaunas' culinary heritage.
fine_dining.local_tips: Don’t miss their cold beetroot soup and cepelinai.

casual_dining.name: Višta Puode
casual_dining.description: Comfortable spot offering hearty Lithuanian meals with a modern twist.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try their honey-glazed chicken and homemade rye bread.

casual_dining.name: Etno Dvaras
casual_dining.description: Serving authentic Lithuanian dishes in a lively atmosphere with folk decor.
casual_dining.local_tips: Pair your meal with traditional Lithuanian mead.

casual_dining.name: Džiaugsmas
casual_dining.description: A modern bistro focusing on local and seasonal ingredients with creative preparations.
casual_dining.local_tips: Ask for the tasting portion to sample more dishes.

casual_dining.name: Casa La Familia
casual_dining.description: Casual dining venue offering a mix of European dishes with Lithuanian influences.
casual_dining.local_tips: Great option for vegetarians with several plant-based dishes.

casual_dining.name: Šnekutis
casual_dining.description: A popular tavern serving classic Lithuanian comfort food and local beers.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try the potato pancakes and homemade sausages.

street_food.name: Kaunas Central Market
street_food.description: Bustling market offering fresh produce, local snacks, and traditional street eats.
street_food.local_tips: Sample kepta duona (fried bread) with garlic sauce from market stalls.

street_food.name: Čili Pica Food Trucks
street_food.description: Popular food trucks serving Lithuanian-style pizzas and fast bites.
street_food.local_tips: Try the traditional mushroom and bacon pizza.

street_food.name: Skanūs skanumynai
street_food.description: Street vendor known for traditional Lithuanian sweet treats like šakotis and meduoliai.
street_food.local_tips: Try their fresh honey cakes during winter markets.

street_food.name: Kaunas Food Hall
street_food.cuisine_type: Various local and international street food vendors
street_food.description: Indoor food hall with multiple stalls offering diverse street food options from Lithuanian to global cuisines.
street_food.local_tips: Great spot to try cepelinai and other Lithuanian staples in casual style.

bars.name: Alaus Biblioteka
bars.description: Extensive selection of Lithuanian craft beers in a cozy, library-inspired setting.
bars.local_tips: Try rare local brews and ask bartenders for lesser-known Lithuanian beers.

bars.name: Papas Baras
bars.description: Trendy cocktail bar with expertly crafted drinks and a lively atmosphere.
bars.local_tips: Book ahead on weekends and try their signature herbal cocktails.

bars.name: Laisvės Alus
bars.description: Modern brewery and pub serving house-made beers and elevated Lithuanian pub fare.
bars.atmosphere: Lively with a modern industrial vibe
bars.local_tips: Order a flight of their seasonal beers to sample variety.

cafes.name: Coffee1
cafes.description: Popular café known for quality single-origin coffees and expert baristas.
cafes.local_tips: Try their pour-over coffee and locally baked pastries.

cafes.name: Chaika
cafes.description: Hip café with a laid-back vibe serving specialty coffees and vegan treats.
cafes.local_tips: Try their homemade vegan cakes and seasonal lattes.

cafes.name: Cafe Montmartre
cafes.description: French-inspired café offering classic espresso drinks and desserts.
cafes.local_tips: Try the croissants and café au lait.

cafes.name: La Boheme
cafes.description: Specialty coffee shop with a focus on artisan brewing methods and local beans.
cafes.local_tips: Ask for their seasonal cold brews in summer.

food_experiences.name: Kaunas Food Tour
food_experiences.description: Guided walking tour sampling traditional Lithuanian dishes and learning about local food culture.
food_experiences.local_tips: Wear comfortable shoes and come hungry to sample a variety of dishes.

food_experiences.name: Traditional Cepelinai Cooking Class
food_experiences.description: Hands-on class learning to prepare Lithuania’s signature potato dumplings with local cooks.
food_experiences.local_tips: Book in advance to secure a spot and enjoy a communal meal after cooking.

food_experiences.name: Kaunas Central Market Visit
food_experiences.description: Explore the vibrant market with a guide, sampling fresh produce, smoked meats, and Lithuanian specialties.
food_experiences.local_tips: Visit early in the morning to see the market at its liveliest.

food_experiences.name: Mushroom Foraging and Cooking Experience
food_experiences.description: Seasonal guided foraging trips followed by a cooking session featuring wild mushrooms.
food_experiences.local_tips: Best booked in autumn; dress appropriately for forest walks.

autumn: Mushroom dishes using forest foraged varieties
winter: Warm barley porridge with dried fruits

transport_types.notes: Frequent direct buses, convenient for same-day trips.
Fastest way; flights from Kaunas Airport.
Direct flights from Kaunas Airport; budget options.
Direct bus connection, scenic Baltic route.
No direct ferry from Kaunas; travel via Riga or Tallinn airport.
Limited flights due to geopolitical situation; check current status.
Limited direct flights; often connections via Warsaw.
Long bus route, overnight options available.
Includes ferry crossing from Tallinn to Helsinki.
Travel from Tallinn to Helsinki after bus connection.

Spring.name: Kaunas Spring Festival
Spring.description: A vibrant celebration of music, art, and theater showcasing local and international talent as the city blooms into spring.
Spring.location: Kaunas Old Town and various cultural venues

Spring.name: Walking Tours of Kaunas Old Town
Spring.description: Explore the historic streets and landmarks of Kaunas Old Town with guided walking tours highlighting its rich history and architecture.

Spring.name: Seasonal Food Tasting at Kaunas Market
Spring.description: Sample fresh spring produce and traditional Lithuanian dishes prepared with seasonal ingredients at local markets.

Spring.name: Dzukija National Park Day Trips
Spring.description: Enjoy hiking and bird watching in the nearby Dzukija National Park as nature awakens with spring blooms.
Spring.location: Dzukija National Park, 1.5 hours from Kaunas

Summer.name: Pažaislis Music Festival
Summer.description: An internationally acclaimed classical and jazz music festival held in the stunning Pažaislis Monastery complex.

Summer.name: Outdoor Swimming at Kaunas Lagoon
Summer.description: Swim, kayak, and relax by the scenic Kaunas Lagoon, a popular spot for water sports and sunbathing during warm summer days.

Summer.name: Kaunas Food Festival
Summer.description: Celebrate Lithuanian cuisine with street food vendors, tastings, and workshops featuring local chefs and fresh summer ingredients.

Summer.name: Cycling along Nemunas River
Summer.description: Take advantage of long daylight hours to cycle scenic routes along the Nemunas River, enjoying nature and city views.

Summer.name: Open-Air Cinema Nights
Summer.description: Watch classic and contemporary films under the stars at open-air cinema events held in parks and squares around the city.

Autumn.name: Kaunas International Film Festival
Autumn.description: Experience a diverse selection of international and Lithuanian films during this well-regarded autumn film festival.
Autumn.location: Kaunas Cinema Centre and cultural venues

Autumn.name: Mushroom Foraging Tours
Autumn.description: Join local guides for mushroom picking excursions in the forests around Kaunas, a traditional autumn activity in Lithuania.

Autumn.name: Autumn Harvest Food Markets
Autumn.description: Taste seasonal Lithuanian produce such as apples, pumpkins, and honey at farmers' markets celebrating the harvest season.

Autumn.name: Strolling through Ąžuolynas Park
Autumn.description: Enjoy the vibrant fall foliage in one of Europe’s largest urban oak parks, perfect for peaceful walks and photography.

Autumn.name: Kaunas Jazz Autumn Sessions
Autumn.description: Attend intimate jazz concerts featuring local and international artists as part of the city’s autumn jazz program.
Autumn.location: Kaunas Jazz Club and cultural centers

Winter.name: Kaunas Christmas Market
Winter.description: Browse festive stalls offering handmade crafts, warm drinks, and traditional Lithuanian holiday treats in a cozy atmosphere.

Winter.name: Ice Skating at Žalgiris Arena
Winter.description: Enjoy public ice skating sessions and hockey matches at the modern Žalgiris Arena during the winter months.

Winter.name: Visiting the Devil’s Museum
Winter.description: Explore this unique museum with a fascinating collection of devil-themed art and sculptures, a warm indoor activity for cold days.

Winter.name: Traditional Lithuanian Winter Cuisine
Winter.description: Savor hearty Lithuanian winter dishes like cepelinai (potato dumplings) and kugelis (potato pudding) at local restaurants.

Winter.name: New Year’s Eve Celebrations in Laisvės Alėja
Winter.description: Join lively public celebrations with live music, fireworks, and festive gatherings to ring in the New Year.

ranges.notes: Cold winter with temperatures often below -5°C; festive New Year and Orthodox Christmas celebrations. Low tourist crowds but atmospheric winter charm.
Cold and often snowy; fewer tourists; many attractions indoors, but limited daylight hours and chilly weather reduce outdoor activities.
Still deep winter with short days; good for budget travelers and winter sports enthusiasts; limited outdoor sightseeing due to cold.
Cold and quiet; museums and cafes are main attractions; low crowds but weather can be harsh and limiting.
Coldest month with frequent snow; outdoor activities limited; low tourist volume; good deals on accommodation.

ranges.event: Kaunas International Film Festival (dates vary, often February)
ranges.notes: Cultural event improves experience despite cold weather; indoor activities highlighted; still low crowds.
Very cold, limited daylight; best for travelers who enjoy winter solitude and low prices.
Slightly improving weather, still cold; good for cultural sightseeing and enjoying winter landscapes.
End of winter; temperatures start to rise but weather remains unpredictable; moderate crowds; good for museums and early spring walks.
Moderate weather; city hosts small celebrations; crowds remain low; good for cultural visits.
Spring begins; weather gradually warms; some rain; outdoor activities become more viable.
Warmer temperatures and longer days; nature starts blooming; good time for sightseeing with fewer tourists than summer.
Spring in full bloom; mild temperatures; increasing daylight; great for parks and outdoor cafes; moderate crowds.
Local Easter traditions and markets add cultural value; some increase in crowds; pleasant spring weather.
Good weather and moderate tourist numbers; ideal for walking tours and visiting cultural sites.
Spring warmth continues; comfortable for outdoor activities; moderate crowds and good value.

ranges.event: Labor Day & St. John’s Day Celebrations
ranges.notes: Warm spring weather with blooming nature; popular holidays increase local festivities; excellent for outdoor events and sightseeing.
Ideal weather with mild temperatures; outdoor cafes and parks lively; beginning of high season; good for all traveler types.
Major cultural event with concerts and street festivals; excellent weather and lively atmosphere; higher crowds but great experience.
Warm and pleasant; perfect for outdoor exploration and cultural activities; tourist numbers growing but manageable.
Start of summer; long daylight hours (up to 17h); warm temperatures ideal for outdoor activities and festivals; moderate to high crowds.

ranges.event: Kaunas Jazz Festival (early June some years)
ranges.notes: World-class jazz festival; warm weather; lively city atmosphere; attracts music lovers and cultural travelers.
Peak summer weather; great for exploring parks, riverside, and outdoor dining; tourist numbers peak but city remains manageable.
Traditional Lithuanian Midsummer celebrations with bonfires and cultural events; warm weather; very lively with high crowds.
Warmest month, temperatures around 20-25°C; ideal for outdoor festivals, river activities, and sightseeing; high tourist volume.

ranges.event: Kaunas Street Music Day (early July some years)
ranges.notes: City-wide music event with live street performances; excellent summer weather; very vibrant atmosphere.
Continued summer warmth; perfect for enjoying outdoor cafes, river activities, and cultural sites; high crowds persist.
Warm and sunny; ideal for festivals, outdoor dining, and sightseeing; peak tourist season with lively atmosphere.
Warm summer weather continues; popular time for local and international tourists; outdoor events and river beaches busy.
Somewhat cooler and higher rainfall possible; still good for outdoor activities; slightly lower crowds than July.

ranges.event: Kaunas Food Festival (mid August some years)
ranges.notes: Food and cultural festival attracts locals and tourists; pleasant weather; crowds moderate to high.
Late summer warmth; good for outdoor sightseeing; tourist numbers start to drop slightly; good value compared to July.
Early autumn; mild temperatures around 15-20°C; fewer tourists; excellent for cultural tours and outdoor walks.
Evening cultural events in museums attract visitors; mild weather; moderate crowds.
Pleasant weather continues; good for outdoor and indoor activities; tourist numbers diminish steadily.
Cooler weather with occasional rain; outdoor activities less comfortable; good for cultural visits and lower crowds.
Autumn colors peak early in the month; cooler and wetter; moderate crowds; some indoor events begin.
Weather turns chillier; fewer tourists; good for cultural activities and museum visits; early evenings.
Increasingly cold and wet; outdoor sightseeing less appealing; low tourist numbers; good deals on accommodation.
Cold and often rainy; indoor attractions recommended; very low crowds; budget travelers benefit from low prices.
Cold and damp; few tourists; many outdoor sites closed or less appealing; indoor cultural venues active.
Increasingly cold with short daylight; low tourist activity; good for budget travelers and indoor cultural experiences.

ranges.event: All Saints' Day (November 1) and Remembrance Day (November 23)
ranges.notes: Local commemorations add cultural depth; generally cold; moderate indoor event attendance; low tourist numbers.
Very cold and dark; low visitor numbers; best for travelers interested in museums and local life off-season.
Cold with increasing festive decorations; Christmas markets start; moderate crowds on weekends; atmospheric winter cityscape.
Christmas markets and events grow; cold weather; good for cultural experiences and holiday atmosphere.
Festive market in full swing; cold but charming atmosphere; increased visitors; great for families and couples.
Holiday season with festive events, local traditions, and New Year's celebrations; cold weather; high demand and lively atmosphere.
